context: Agent must correctly identify that no pattern is warranted for a single-method, single-implementation class and articulate the anti-premature-pattern principle

checklist.name: Problem statement describes no concrete problem
checklist.description: The Problem Statement section correctly notes there is no current conditional logic, coupling problem, or varying algorithm — the code is a simple calculation

checklist.name: Win condition correctly evaluated as unmet
checklist.description: The Pattern Fit Assessment explicitly states the Strategy win condition (eliminating if/else selection between algorithms) is not met because only one implementation exists

checklist.name: Recommendation is to defer
checklist.description: The Recommendation section recommends NOT applying the Strategy pattern now

checklist.name: Anti-preemptive principle referenced
checklist.description: The recommendation references the principle of not applying patterns for imagined future needs (YAGNI, or equivalent statement about waiting for concrete need)

checklist.name: Trigger condition is concrete and correct
checklist.description: The Trigger Condition describes a concrete code event (e.g., 'when a second tax type with a different formula must be added and selected conditionally') that would justify the pattern
